title: Operator TUI: Carbonyl WebRTC/DataChannel session with OIDC and modern browser capabilities
summary: Add an explicit WebRTC-capable Carbonyl center-browser session for Ananta. Primary goal is RTCDataChannel-based peer/session communication and artifact exchange inside an authenticated browser session. Audio/video and camera/microphone are optional capability probes and disabled by default.
design_principle: WebRTC is not just rendering. It is a network/session/runtime feature. Carbonyl may host the browser page in the CenterViewport, but Ananta must own policy, identity binding, signaling configuration, permissions and audit state.
intended_use_cases: Authenticated peer-to-peer Ananta session using Keycloak/OIDC identity.
DataChannel ping/pong and low-latency control messages.
Encrypted artifact/chunk exchange between trusted peers.
Collaborative TUI/session demo through webrtc.ananta.de signaling.
Optional media capability smoke test for microphone/camera/audio/video where supported.
non_goals: Do not enable camera or microphone by default.
Do not assume WSL2 terminal device access works for media.
Do not use WebRTC as a replacement for OIDC identity proof.
Do not allow arbitrary remote signaling servers without allowlist.
Do not persist received artifacts without explicit policy decision.

components.name: CarbonylBrowserModeController
components.responsibility: Launches Carbonyl in the CenterViewport and loads the local WebRTC app or authenticated remote app.

components.name: OidcAuthController
components.responsibility: Provides login/session identity before WebRTC session start.

components.name: WebRtcSessionController
components.responsibility: Owns signaling state, peer identity binding, channel lifecycle and policy decisions.

components.name: SignalingClient
components.responsibility: Connects to configured signaling endpoint such as wss://webrtc.ananta.de/signaling.

components.name: DataChannelProtocol
components.responsibility: Defines structured messages for ping/pong, control events, artifact metadata and chunk transfer.

components.name: PermissionPolicy
components.responsibility: Controls datachannel, remote URL, camera, microphone, file/artifact exchange and TURN usage.

default_flow: User explicitly starts WebRTC mode from Operator TUI.
Ananta verifies OIDC session or triggers Carbonyl OIDC login first.
Carbonyl loads local Ananta WebRTC test/app page in CenterViewport.
Web app connects to allowlisted signaling server.
Peers exchange offers/answers/ICE candidates via signaling.
DataChannel opens and runs capability handshake.
Artifact exchange is allowed only after identity and policy checks.
Leaving WebRTC mode closes channels, stops signaling and returns to previous CenterView.

implementation_tasks.title: Create local WebRTC browser app loaded by Carbonyl
details: Create a minimal local HTML/JS app for WebRTC testing and runtime use.
Load only local JS/CSS by default.
Render session status: OIDC state, signaling state, ICE state, DataChannel state, peer id and degraded reasons.
Expose a safe postMessage or localhost bridge only if needed and policy-controlled.
acceptance_criteria: Carbonyl can open the local WebRTC app from CenterViewport.
The app performs no remote fetch before the user starts/connects WebRTC.
The app clearly shows disconnected, connecting, connected and failed states.
Default app works without camera/microphone permission requests.

implementation_tasks.title: Implement allowlisted signaling client
details: Support configured WebSocket signaling server URLs only.
Reject signaling servers not present in allowed_servers.
Define message envelope for join, leave, offer, answer, ice_candidate, error and heartbeat.
Include Ananta session id, OIDC subject hash and session nonce where appropriate, but never raw tokens.
Add reconnect=false as default to avoid accidental long-running sessions.
acceptance_criteria: Unallowlisted signaling URL is rejected before any network connection.
Offer/answer/candidate messages serialize and deserialize deterministically.
Raw OIDC tokens are never included in signaling messages.
Timeout/failure returns a structured degraded state to the TUI.

implementation_tasks.title: Bridge Carbonyl browser app and Ananta runtime safely
details: Decide whether signaling is performed in the browser JS app or in Python runtime; document the chosen boundary.
If browser owns RTCPeerConnection, pass only sanitized config and session metadata to the page.
If Python owns signaling, expose minimal localhost endpoint for signaling relay with CSRF/session nonce protection.
Keep OIDC tokens in Ananta runtime; browser receives only derived non-secret session identity claims.
acceptance_criteria: The browser app can establish a DataChannel without receiving provider access/refresh tokens.
Session nonce is required for local bridge calls.
Invalid bridge calls are rejected and audited.
Boundary decision is documented in docs/operator-tui/carbonyl-webrtc-session.md.

implementation_tasks.title: Define and implement Ananta DataChannel protocol v1
details: Define JSON message envelope: type, protocol_version, session_nonce, message_id, timestamp, payload.
Initial message types: hello, hello_ack, ping, pong, artifact_offer, artifact_accept, artifact_reject, artifact_chunk, artifact_complete, error.
Require SHA-256 integrity for artifact transfer.
Use chunking for large artifacts and application-level acknowledgements.
Add max sizes and reject oversize messages before processing payload.
acceptance_criteria: Ping/pong works over DataChannel in a local smoke test.
Artifact transfer verifies final SHA-256 before marking complete.
Oversized messages/chunks are rejected safely.
Protocol version mismatch produces a clear error message.

implementation_tasks.title: Bind WebRTC peer/session to OIDC identity and Ananta policy
details: Require an authenticated Ananta/OIDC session before starting WebRTC by default.
Bind peer claims to OIDC subject or provider-specific stable id without exposing raw tokens.
Use explicit accept policy for artifact exchange.
Add deny-by-default for microphone, camera and screen share.
Add policy reason strings for user-visible degraded/denied states.
acceptance_criteria: Starting WebRTC without login triggers login flow or fails closed depending on config.
Unknown peer identity is rejected by default.
Artifact exchange requires explicit local approval unless policy is changed.
Camera/microphone requests are blocked by default.

implementation_tasks.title: Add ICE/STUN/TURN capability and connectivity probes
details: Add smoke test for ICE gathering against configured STUN/TURN servers.
Report host/srflx/relay candidate categories without leaking exact local IPs in normal logs.
Test TURN fallback with ephemeral credentials where available.
Report unsupported browser/WebRTC API states clearly.
acceptance_criteria: Smoke script can run in mock mode without real network.
With configured servers, script reports whether relay candidates are available.
Normal logs redact precise candidate IPs unless verbose-debug is explicitly enabled.
Failure does not leave WebSocket or Carbonyl processes running.

implementation_tasks.title: Add optional audio/video/camera/microphone capability experiment
details: Keep media disabled by default.
Provide explicit command to run media probe.
Probe navigator.mediaDevices availability and permission behavior in Carbonyl/terminal/WSL2.
Do not request camera/microphone unless the user explicitly selected media probe.
Document expected degraded behavior under WSL2 and headless/terminal environments.
acceptance_criteria: Default WebRTC DataChannel test does not trigger camera/microphone permission prompt.
Media probe clearly reports unsupported, permission_denied, device_missing or success.
Failed media probe does not affect DataChannel functionality.
Docs warn that media support is experimental in Carbonyl terminal mode.

implementation_tasks.title: Add TUI controls/status for WebRTC mode
details: Add commands center.browser.webrtc.start, center.browser.webrtc.stop, center.browser.webrtc.status and center.browser.webrtc.accept_artifact.
Show concise status line: auth, signaling, ICE, DataChannel, peer, transfer state.
Do not reuse F8/F9/F10; keep browser/WebRTC controls conflict-free.
Esc exits active Carbonyl/WebRTC mode and closes connections gracefully.
acceptance_criteria: User can start/stop WebRTC mode explicitly.
Status line shows disconnected/connecting/connected/failed states.
Esc closes channels and returns to previous CenterView.
Keybinding conflict detection covers new commands.

implementation_tasks.title: Audit and cleanup WebRTC sessions safely
details: Emit structured audit events for session_start, signaling_connected, peer_offer_received, peer_accepted, datachannel_open, artifact_offer, artifact_complete, session_closed and error.
Do not log artifact payloads, raw tokens, cookies or full ICE candidate IPs by default.
Ensure cleanup closes DataChannel, RTCPeerConnection, WebSocket signaling and Carbonyl subprocess where owned.
Add timeout cleanup for half-open sessions.
acceptance_criteria: Audit output contains enough state for debugging but no sensitive payload values.
Tests fail if token-like or cookie-like values are logged.
Closing WebRTC mode leaves no open signaling connection.
Half-open sessions are cleaned after timeout.

implementation_tasks.title: Document WebRTC + OIDC + Carbonyl setup
details: Explain the difference between Carbonyl as renderer, Carbonyl as OIDC browser session and Carbonyl as WebRTC app host.
Document setup for keycloak.ananta.de and webrtc.ananta.de as limited public test infrastructure.
Warn that public Ananta test services are rate/usage limited and not for production.
Provide local-only mock signaling example for development.
Include Mermaid diagrams for OIDC-before-WebRTC and DataChannel artifact transfer.
acceptance_criteria: Docs allow a developer to run a local mock WebRTC DataChannel smoke test.
Docs explain required Keycloak/OIDC config and redirect URIs.
Docs clearly mark audio/video as experimental.
Docs clearly mark webrtc.ananta.de/keycloak.ananta.de as limited test services.

mermaid_architecture:
flowchart TD
    A[User starts WebRTC mode in Operator TUI] --> B{OIDC session exists?}
    B -->|no| C[Carbonyl OIDC login flow]
    B -->|yes| D[Load local WebRTC app in Carbonyl]
    C --> D
    D --> E[Connect to allowlisted signaling server]
    E --> F[Exchange offer answer ICE]
    F --> G[RTCPeerConnection]
    G --> H[RTCDataChannel]
    H --> I[hello + identity/session nonce handshake]
    I --> J{Policy allows artifact exchange?}
    J -->|yes explicit accept| K[Chunked artifact transfer + SHA256 verify]
    J -->|no| L[Reject with reason]
    K --> M[Audit redacted completion]
    L --> M
    N[Esc/stop] --> O[Close channel peer connection signaling Carbonyl mode]
acceptance_summary: WebRTC mode is explicit-only and requires OIDC session by default.
DataChannel ping/pong works before any artifact exchange feature is considered complete.
Signaling server is allowlisted and defaults to webrtc.ananta.de only when configured.
Artifact exchange is integrity-checked and explicit-accept by default.
Camera/microphone/screen-share are disabled by default and only available as experimental probes.
Leaving the mode cleans up Carbonyl, signaling, PeerConnection and channels.
